sets.title: A New Hobby
paragraphs: Last year I decided to (1) ___ up photography as a hobby. I borrowed my dad's old camera and started taking photos (2) ___ my neighbourhood. At first my photos were not very good, but I kept practising.
I watched videos online (3) ___ taught me about lighting and composition. Slowly my pictures began to improve. Now I enter (4) ___ school competitions and have already won one prize.
Photography has helped me see the world (5) ___ differently. I notice small details (6) ___ I never noticed before, like the way light reflects on a puddle or how shadows fall on walls.

gaps.rationale: 'Take up' is the fixed phrasal verb meaning to start a new hobby.
'Around my neighbourhood' or 'in my neighbourhood' are both correct.
Relative pronoun referring to the videos — 'that' or 'which'.
'Enter some competitions' — 'some' is the natural quantifier here.
'See the world so/completely differently' expresses a change in perspective.
Relative clause referring to details — 'that' or 'which'.

sets.title: My Best Friend
paragraphs: I have known my best friend Sara (1) ___ we were seven years old. We met at primary school and quickly became friends because (2) ___ of us liked the same things: books, music and animals.
We do not go to (3) ___ same school any more because her family moved to another part of the city. However, we still see each other (4) ___ the weekend and chat online almost every day.
I think a good friendship is (5) ___ on trust and honesty. Sara always tells me the truth, even when I do not want to hear it. That is (6) ___ I think she is my best friend.

gaps.rationale: 'Known her since we were seven' — 'since' introduces the starting point.
'Both of us liked the same things' — 'both' refers to the two friends.
Definite article before 'same school'.
'See each other at the weekend' — 'at' is used with 'weekend' in British English.
'Based on trust and honesty' — the correct participle adjective.
'That is why I think she is my best friend' — reason connector.

sets.title: My School Day
paragraphs: I usually wake (1) ___ at half past six and get ready for school. I have breakfast (2) ___ my family before leaving the house at quarter past seven.
School starts at eight o'clock. (3) ___ the morning we have four lessons. We have a twenty-minute break at ten thirty and then two more lessons before lunch.

I eat in the school canteen with my friends. In (4) ___ afternoon we have two more lessons and then we finish at four o'clock. After school I go (5) ___ to the library to do my homework. I try to finish (6) ___ before dinner so I have time to relax in the evening.

gaps.rationale: 'Wake up' — fixed phrasal verb meaning to stop sleeping.
'Have breakfast with my family' — 'with' for shared activity.
'In the morning' — standard time expression.
'In the afternoon' — definite article before 'afternoon'.
'Go straight to the library' or 'go back to the library'.
'Finish it before dinner' — 'it' refers to the homework.

sets.title: The School Play
paragraphs: Every year our school puts (1) ___ a play for parents and students. This year we are performing a comedy, which is (2) ___ harder to act than a serious drama because the timing of each joke must be perfect.

I am playing one (3) ___ the main characters. I have been learning my lines for (4) ___ months and I still make mistakes sometimes. My drama teacher says the most important thing is not to stop (5) ___ you forget a word — just keep going and the audience will not notice.
The performance is next Friday. I am a (6) ___ nervous but also very excited about it.

gaps.rationale: 'Put on a play' — fixed phrasal verb meaning to organise a performance.
'Much harder' or 'a lot harder' — comparative with uncountable degree.
'One of the main characters' — partitive structure.
Any plural count indicating several months of practice.
'Don't stop if/when you forget a word' — conditional or temporal.
'A little nervous' or 'a bit nervous' — both are natural hedges.

sets.title: Healthy Eating at School
paragraphs: Our school has recently (1) ___ changes to its canteen menu. There are (2) ___ more vegetables, salads and fruit available, and less fried food.
Some students were not happy (3) ___ first because they liked the old menu. However, most people now agree that the food tastes better (4) ___ it is fresher.

The school also runs a programme to teach students (5) ___ to cook simple healthy meals. Students learn how to prepare dishes using ingredients (6) ___ are in season, which means they are usually cheaper and more nutritious.

gaps.rationale: 'Made changes' — 'make changes' collocates; past participle after 'has'.
'Now more vegetables' or 'many more vegetables' — quantity or recency.
'Not happy at first' — 'at first' is a fixed adverbial phrase.
Causal connectors — 'because/as/since it is fresher'.
'Teach students how to cook' — indirect question with 'how'.
Relative pronoun for 'ingredients that/which are in season'.

sets.title: Learning a Language
paragraphs: I have been learning French (1) ___ three years. I started because I wanted to speak to my grandmother, (2) ___ moved to France when she was young. She is very pleased that I am learning her language.
At school we do reading, writing and listening exercises. (3) ___ my opinion, speaking is the most difficult skill because you have to think quickly and there is no time to check a dictionary.
I try to practise as (4) ___ as possible by watching French films and listening (5) ___ French music. I also chat online (6) ___ a French pen pal I found through a language exchange website.

gaps.rationale: 'Learning French for three years' — 'for' expresses duration.
Relative pronoun referring to grandmother — subject of the clause.
'In my opinion' — fixed expression.
'As much as possible' or 'as often as possible' — both natural here.
'Listening to French music' — 'listen to' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'Chat with a pen pal' — 'with' for interacting with someone.

sets.title: Weekend in the Mountains
paragraphs: Last weekend my family and I went (1) ___ a camping trip in the mountains. We drove for about two hours (2) ___ we arrived at the campsite.

We set (3) ___ our tent and cooked dinner over an open fire. It was much colder than I expected, (4) ___ we had sleeping bags and warm clothes. In the morning we went on a long walk through the forest.
I am not usually (5) ___ of outdoor activities, but this trip was really special. I am already (6) ___ forward to the next one.

gaps.rationale: 'Go on a camping trip' — 'on' is fixed with 'trip'.
'Drove for two hours before/until we arrived' — temporal connector.
'Set up a tent' — fixed phrasal verb.
'Colder than expected, but we had sleeping bags' — contrast connector.
'Keen on outdoor activities' or 'fond of outdoor activities'.
'Looking forward to the next one' — fixed phrase with 'look forward to'.

sets.title: Being a Good Student
paragraphs: Being a good student does not (1) ___ mean getting top marks in every test. It also means being curious, asking questions and (2) ___ your best even when something is difficult.
Good students usually have a regular study routine and make (3) ___ of class time. They also ask for help (4) ___ they do not understand something rather than pretending they do.

It is important to look (5) ___ your physical and mental health too. If you are tired or stressed, it is hard to concentrate. Getting enough sleep and taking breaks (6) ___ study helps your brain work better.

gaps.rationale: 'Does not just mean' — 'just' narrows the meaning.
'Doing your best' or 'trying your best' — gerund as subject complement.
'Make use of class time' — fixed phrasal noun.
'Ask for help when/if they don't understand' — conditional/temporal.
'Look after your health' — phrasal verb meaning take care of.
'Taking breaks from study' — 'from' indicates the activity paused.

sets.title: A Special Teacher
paragraphs: When I think (1) ___ the teachers who have influenced me most, I always think of my primary school English teacher, Ms Patel. She made every lesson (2) ___ interesting and fun.

Ms Patel was always patient and never made us (3) ___ embarrassed if we made a mistake. She believed that mistakes were part of learning. She encouraged us to read books that we enjoyed (4) ___ of books we had to read for school.
Because of her, I developed a love (5) ___ reading and writing that I still have today. I sometimes wonder (6) ___ would have happened if I had not had such a great teacher.

gaps.rationale: 'Think about' or 'think of' teachers — both correct.
'Made every lesson feel/seem interesting' — causative with adjective.
'Made us feel embarrassed' — causative construction.
'Instead of books we had to read' — fixed preposition phrase.
'A love of reading' or 'a love for reading' — both accepted.
'Wonder what would have happened' — embedded question with 'what'.

sets.title: Climate Change and Young People
paragraphs: Many young people (1) ___ the world are concerned about climate change. They want governments and companies to take action (2) ___ protect the environment for future generations.

Some teenagers show their concern by joining environmental groups or taking (3) ___ in protests. Others focus on making small changes in (4) ___ own lives, such as using less plastic, cycling instead of travelling by car, and buying second-hand clothes.

Experts say that young people's voices (5) ___ important because they will live with the consequences of climate change for longer than older people. Every action, (6) ___ small, can make a difference.

gaps.rationale: 'Around the world' or 'across the world' — both mean globally.
'Take action to protect' — infinitive of purpose.
'Taking part in protests' — 'take part in' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'In their own lives' — possessive pronoun referring back to teenagers.
'Voices are important' — plural subject needs plural verb.
'However small' or 'no matter how small' — concessive clause.

sets.title: My Favourite Subject
paragraphs: My favourite subject at school is science. I have always been interested (1) ___ how things work, so science lessons feel like exciting discoveries. Our teacher does experiments (2) ___ us so we can see the ideas in action.

Last month we learned (3) ___ plants make food from sunlight. I found it amazing that something so small as a leaf can do something so complex. I decided to grow some bean plants at home to (4) ___ what I had learned.
I hope to study science (5) ___ university one day. My dream job is to work (6) ___ a scientist and carry out research that helps people.

gaps.rationale: 'Interested in how things work' — 'interested in' is the fixed collocation.
'Does experiments for us' or 'does experiments with us' — both natural.
'Learned how plants make food' — indirect question introduced by 'how'.
'To test/try what I had learned' — apply knowledge practically.
'Study science at university' — 'at' is fixed with 'university' in British English.
'Work as a scientist' — 'as' introduces the role or profession.

sets.title: A Trip to the Zoo
paragraphs: Last Saturday our class went (1) ___ a trip to the city zoo. We travelled there (2) ___ bus and arrived just after nine o'clock in the morning.

The zoo is home to (3) ___ than two hundred species of animals from all over the world. My favourite was the gorilla enclosure because the gorillas looked almost human. One of them stared (4) ___ me for a long time and I felt a little nervous.
We also visited the reptile house, (5) ___ I found quite frightening. At the end of the visit, a guide told us about the zoo's programme to protect (6) ___ species that are close to extinction.

gaps.rationale: 'Go on a trip' — 'on' is fixed with 'trip'.
'Travelled by bus' — 'by' introduces the means of transport.
'More than two hundred species' — 'more than' for exceeding a number.
'Stared at me' — 'stare at' is the fixed phrasal verb.
Non-defining relative clause after a comma — 'which' is required.
'Endangered species' is the natural collocation in conservation contexts.

sets.title: Helping My Family
paragraphs: I try to help (1) ___ home as much as I can. Every morning I make my bed and tidy my room before going to school. At weekends I help my mum (2) ___ the shopping.

My favourite chore is cooking. I started learning to cook (3) ___ I was ten, and now I can make several simple meals on my own. Last week I cooked pasta for the whole family and everyone said it tasted (4) ___.
I think it is important for young people to (5) ___ responsibility at home. It teaches you useful skills and makes you appreciate (6) ___ much work your parents do every day.

gaps.rationale: 'Help at home' — 'at home' is the fixed locative phrase.
'Help with the shopping' or 'help do the shopping' — both correct at A2.
'Started learning when I was ten' — 'when' introduces the time clause.
Any positive adjective after a link verb ('tasted + adjective') works here.
'Take responsibility' — fixed collocation.
'Appreciate how much work your parents do' — indirect question with 'how'.

sets.title: My Favourite Sport
paragraphs: I have played basketball (1) ___ I was nine years old. My older brother taught me the basics, and I quickly fell (2) ___ love with the sport.
I train (3) ___ a week with my school team. Our coach is very strict but also very encouraging. She says that (4) ___ you work hard enough, you can always improve.
Last season we won the regional championship. It was the proudest moment of my life. Playing basketball has taught me a lot (5) ___ teamwork and discipline. I cannot imagine my life (6) ___ it.

gaps.rationale: 'Played since I was nine' — 'since' introduces the starting point of a period.
'Fell in love with the sport' — 'fall in love with' is a fixed expression.
Frequency expression before 'a week' — 'twice a week' or 'three times a week'.
'If you work hard enough' — conditional clause.
'Taught me a lot about teamwork' — 'about' introduces the topic learned.
'Cannot imagine my life without it' — 'without' expresses absence.

sets.title: Getting a New Pet
paragraphs: For my birthday last month my parents gave (1) ___ a puppy. I had always wanted a dog, so I was absolutely delighted. We called him Pip because he made a little pip sound (2) ___ he was happy.
Looking (3) ___ a puppy is hard work. You have to feed him several times a day, take him for walks and train him not to chew things. At first Pip was very naughty and chewed (4) ___ of my shoes!
Now Pip is better behaved and we have become very close. I take him for a walk every morning (5) ___ school. Having a pet has made me more responsible and (6) ___ my time more carefully.

gaps.rationale: 'Gave me a puppy' — indirect object pronoun.
'When he was happy' — temporal clause.
'Looking after a puppy' — 'look after' means to take care of.
'Chewed one of my shoes' — partitive with a countable noun.
'Every morning before school' — 'before' introduces the time reference.
'Manage my time more carefully' — 'manage time' is the natural collocation.

sets.title: My Home Town
paragraphs: I come (1) ___ a small town called Riverdale in the north of the country. About twenty thousand people live there, which means everyone knows (2) ___ other.
There is not much to do for young people, but I like the (3) ___ that it is quiet and safe. There is a river running through the centre and I enjoy walking (4) ___ its banks in the evening.
Some of my friends want to move to a big city (5) ___ they finish school, but I am not sure I would want to. I love my town and I think I will always feel a strong connection (6) ___ it.

gaps.rationale: 'Come from a town' — 'come from' expresses origin.
'Everyone knows each other' — 'each other' is the fixed reciprocal pronoun.
'Like the fact that it is quiet' — 'the fact that' introduces a nominal clause.
'Walking along its banks' — 'along' describes movement beside a linear feature.
'When/after/once they finish school' — temporal connectors.
'A strong connection to it' — 'connection to' is the fixed preposition.

sets.title: Learning to Swim
paragraphs: I learned to swim (1) ___ the age of six at a local swimming pool. My mum took me to lessons every Saturday morning (2) ___ a whole year.
At first I was very (3) ___ of the water. I clung to the side of the pool and refused to let go. My instructor was patient and encouraged me to try (4) ___ and harder each week.
By the end of the year I could swim twenty lengths without stopping. I was so proud (5) ___ myself. Now I swim (6) ___ relaxation and I feel completely comfortable in the water.

gaps.rationale: 'At the age of six' — 'at' is fixed with 'age'.
'Every Saturday for a whole year' — 'for' expresses duration.
'Very afraid/scared/frightened of the water' — adjective + 'of'.
'Try harder and harder each week' — comparative adverb showing gradual increase.
'Proud of myself' — 'proud of' is the fixed adjective + preposition.
'Swim for relaxation' — 'for' introduces the purpose.

sets.title: My Morning Routine
paragraphs: I always set (1) ___ alarm for six forty-five, but I usually press snooze at least once before I actually get up. Then I go straight to the bathroom to have a shower (2) ___ getting dressed.

Breakfast is the most important part of my morning. I (3) ___ never skip it because I lose concentration during my first lesson if I have not eaten. I usually have cereal or toast (4) ___ a glass of orange juice.
I leave the house at twenty past seven to catch the bus. If I miss it, I have to walk for (5) ___ minutes, which makes me late. That is (6) ___ I always try to leave on time.

gaps.rationale: 'Set my alarm' — possessive pronoun referring to the speaker's alarm.
'Have a shower before getting dressed' — temporal sequence.
'I would/could never skip it' or emphatic 'I do never skip it' — modal or emphatic auxiliary.
'Toast with a glass of orange juice' — 'with' accompanies food items.
Any plausible number of walking minutes to school.
'That is why I always try to leave on time' — reason connector.

sets.title: A Fun School Project
paragraphs: Last term our class worked (1) ___ a project about the history of our town. We were split into groups of four and each group had to research a different period (2) ___ time.
My group chose the nineteen fifties. We visited the local library and looked (3) ___ old newspapers and photographs. It was fascinating to see how different life was back (4) ___.

At the end of the term, each group presented their findings to the rest of the class. Our presentation lasted about twenty minutes. I was nervous (5) ___ first, but once I started speaking I began to enjoy it. Our teacher said she was proud (6) ___ all of us.

gaps.rationale: 'Worked on a project' — 'work on' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'A different period of time' — 'period of time' is a fixed noun phrase.
'Looked at old newspapers' or 'looked through old newspapers' — both correct.
'How different life was back then' — 'back then' refers to a past time.
'Nervous at first' — 'at first' is a fixed adverbial phrase.
'Proud of all of us' — 'proud of' is the fixed adjective + preposition.

sets.title: My First Day at a New School
paragraphs: When I was twelve, my family moved to a new city and I had to start (1) ___ a new school. I felt very nervous the night before because I did not know anyone.

On the first morning, a girl (2) ___ Maria came and sat next to me. She introduced me to her friends at break time and made sure I did not feel (3) ___ out. By lunchtime I was already feeling much better.

By the end of the week I had settled (4) ___ well. I realised that starting somewhere new is not as scary as you think (5) ___ you actually do it. Now I am glad my family moved because I have made (6) ___ really good friends here.

gaps.rationale: 'Start at a new school' — 'at' is fixed with school as an institution.
'A girl called/named Maria' — participle used to identify a person by name.
'Feel left out' — fixed expression meaning excluded.
'Settled in well' — 'settle in' means to become comfortable in a new place.
'Not as scary as you think until/when you actually do it' — temporal connector.
'Made some really good friends' — 'some' is the natural quantifier.

sets.title: Going Camping
paragraphs: Every summer my family goes camping for a week. We usually choose a site near a lake (1) ___ we can swim and fish. This year we went to a forest campsite in the south of the country.
We arrived in the (2) ___ afternoon and spent two hours putting up the tents. My dad is good (3) ___ this because he has done it so many times, but my little brother and I kept getting in the way.

The best part of camping is sitting around the fire (4) ___ the evening, telling stories and eating toasted marshmallows. I always sleep (5) ___ well in a tent because the fresh air makes me tired. I am already looking forward (6) ___ next summer.

gaps.rationale: 'A site where we can swim' — relative adverb referring to the location.
'Arrived in the late afternoon' — adjective modifying 'afternoon'.
'Good at this' — 'be good at' is the fixed adjective + preposition.
'Sitting around the fire in the evening' — 'in the evening' is a fixed time expression.
Intensifier before 'well' — 'sleep so/really well'.
'Looking forward to next summer' — 'look forward to' requires 'to'.

sets.title: A Surprise Party
paragraphs: Last month I organised a surprise birthday party (1) ___ my mum. It was her fiftieth birthday, so I wanted to do something really special. I secretly asked all her friends and family to come (2) ___ giving her any warning.
I spent two weeks planning everything. I ordered a cake, made (3) ___ of the decorations myself and created a playlist of her favourite songs. On the day, I told Mum we were going out (4) ___ dinner.
When she walked (5) ___ to find all her friends there, she burst into tears. She said it was the best surprise she had ever had. Seeing how happy she was made (6) ___ feel wonderful.

gaps.rationale: 'Organised a party for my mum' — 'for' indicates who benefits.
'Come without giving her any warning' — 'without' + gerund.
'Made some/most of the decorations myself' — partitive quantifier.
'Going out for dinner' — 'go out for dinner' is a fixed expression.
'Walked in to find all her friends' — 'walk in' means to enter a room.
'Made me feel wonderful' — accusative pronoun as object of 'made'.

sets.title: Learning to Ride a Bike
paragraphs: I learned to ride a bike (1) ___ the summer I was seven. My dad took me to the park every evening and held the back of the saddle (2) ___ I could balance on my own.
After about a week I finally managed to ride a few metres (3) ___ falling off. I was so excited that I shouted and nearly crashed into a tree! My dad laughed and said he was (4) ___ of me.
Now I cycle everywhere. I ride to school, to my friends' houses and to the shops. Cycling is good (5) ___ the environment and good for your health (6) ___ well.

gaps.rationale: 'During/in the summer I was seven' — both prepositions work with a named period.
'Held the saddle until I could balance' — 'until' introduces the end point.
'Ride a few metres without falling off' — 'without' + gerund.
'He was proud of me' — 'be proud of' is fixed; the adjective is 'proud'.
'Good for the environment' — 'good for' is the fixed adjective + preposition.
'Good for your health as well' — 'as well' is a fixed additive phrase.

sets.title: Keeping a Diary
paragraphs: I have kept a diary (1) ___ I was eleven. Every night before bed I write about what happened during the day and how I felt (2) ___ it.

I think writing a diary is a great way to deal (3) ___ stress. When I am upset about something, putting my feelings into words helps me calm (4) ___. It is also interesting to look back at old entries and see how much I have changed.

Some people prefer to type their diary (5) ___ a phone or computer, but I prefer using a notebook. There is something special about writing by hand. I would recommend keeping a diary to (6) ___ who wants to understand themselves better.

gaps.rationale: 'Kept a diary since I was eleven' — 'since' introduces the starting point.
'How I felt about it' — 'feel about' expresses a reaction to something.
'Deal with stress' — 'deal with' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'Helps me calm down' — 'calm down' is the fixed phrasal verb meaning to become less upset.
'Type their diary on a phone or computer' — 'on' with devices.
'Recommend to anyone who wants to understand themselves better' — indefinite pronoun.

sets.title: Cooking with My Parents
paragraphs: Every Sunday my family cooks a big meal together. My parents believe that knowing (1) ___ to cook is an essential life skill, so they started teaching me when I was quite young.

My mum is in (2) ___ of the starters and salads, while my dad is responsible for the main course. I am usually given the task (3) ___ peeling vegetables or stirring sauces. I am slowly learning more difficult techniques.

Last week I cooked the dessert (4) ___ myself for the first time. I made a chocolate cake and it turned (5) ___ really well. My parents said it was delicious, which made me feel very proud (6) ___ myself.

gaps.rationale: 'Knowing how to cook' — 'how to' introduces the indirect question.
'In charge of the starters' — 'in charge of' is a fixed expression meaning responsible for.
'Given the task of peeling' — 'task of' + gerund is fixed.
'Cooked the dessert by myself' — 'by myself' means alone/without help.
'Turned out really well' — 'turn out' is the fixed phrasal verb meaning to have a result.
'Feel very proud of myself' — 'proud of' is the fixed adjective + preposition.

sets.title: A School Competition
paragraphs: Last month I entered a writing competition (1) ___ my school organised. The topic was 'A world without technology' and we had to write a short story of (2) ___ than five hundred words.

I spent three evenings working (3) ___ my story. I wrote about a family that woke up one morning to find all their devices had stopped working. It was difficult to think (4) ___ new ideas, but I kept going.

To my surprise I won second place. The prize was a book token and my story was published in the school (5) ___. My teacher said she was impressed (6) ___ my imagination and my use of descriptive language.

gaps.rationale: Relative pronoun for the competition 'that/which my school organised'.
'No more than five hundred words' — 'more' is the standard word-count limiter.
'Working on my story' — 'work on' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'Difficult to think of new ideas' — 'think of' means to produce or come up with.
School publication — any of these is natural in context.
'Impressed by/with my imagination' — both prepositions follow 'impressed'.

sets.title: My Favourite Season
paragraphs: My favourite season is autumn. I love the way the leaves (1) ___ colour and cover the ground in shades of red, orange and yellow. The air becomes cooler and fresher, (2) ___ makes it perfect for walking.
At the weekends I often go to the park (3) ___ my family. We collect chestnuts and kick piles of leaves. My mum always brings hot chocolate in a flask, (4) ___ keeps us warm.
I also love the smell of autumn. After it rains, the ground gives off a rich, earthy smell (5) ___ I find really relaxing. I think autumn is the most beautiful season (6) ___ all.

gaps.rationale: 'Leaves change colour' — 'change colour' is the fixed collocation.
Non-defining relative clause after a comma — 'which' is required.
'Go to the park with my family' — 'with' for company.
Non-defining relative clause — 'which keeps us warm'.
Relative pronoun referring to 'smell' — 'that/which I find relaxing'.
'The most beautiful season of all' — 'of all' is the fixed superlative intensifier.

sets.title: A Day at the Beach
paragraphs: Last weekend my family drove to the coast for the day. We left early in the morning (1) ___ avoid the traffic and arrived at the beach just after nine.

The weather was perfect — warm and sunny with a light breeze. We spent the morning swimming and building sandcastles. My little sister is (2) ___ good at building sandcastles and hers was the biggest on the beach.

In the afternoon we had lunch at a small café (3) ___ the seafront. I had fish and chips, (4) ___ is a traditional British seaside meal. We stayed until sunset and drove (5) ___ home. By the time we arrived, I was so tired I fell (6) ___ almost immediately.

gaps.rationale: 'Left early to avoid the traffic' — infinitive of purpose.
Intensifier before 'good at' — 'really/so/very good at building'.
'A café on the seafront' — 'on' for location along a seafront or street.
Non-defining relative clause after a comma — 'which is a traditional meal'.
'Drove back home' — 'drive back' means to return by car.
'Fell asleep almost immediately' — 'fall asleep' is the fixed phrasal verb.

sets.title: Making a Birthday Card
paragraphs: My grandma's birthday is next week and I decided to make her a card (1) ___ of buying one in a shop. I think homemade cards are more personal and show that you care.

I went to the craft shop and bought coloured card, glue, ribbons and some small stickers. When I got home I sat (2) ___ at the kitchen table and started designing the card. It took me (3) ___ two hours because I kept changing my mind.
In the end I was really pleased (4) ___ how it looked. I wrote a long message (5) ___ and decorated the envelope too. I cannot wait to see my grandma's face (6) ___ she opens it.

gaps.rationale: 'Instead of buying one in a shop' — 'instead of' + gerund for the alternative.
'Sat down at the kitchen table' — 'sit down' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'Took me about/nearly/almost two hours' — approximation adverb.
'Pleased with how it looked' — 'pleased with' is the fixed adjective + preposition.
'Wrote a long message inside' — 'inside' locates the message within the card.
'Her face when she opens it' — temporal clause.

sets.title: A Family Trip
paragraphs: Last summer my family went (1) ___ holiday to Portugal for two weeks. It was the first time any of us had (2) ___ there, so we were all very excited.

We stayed in a small apartment near the beach. Every morning we had breakfast on the balcony and watched the sun rise (3) ___ the sea. During the day we explored the local villages and tried different kinds of food.
The thing I remember (4) ___ is visiting an old castle on top of a hill. The view from the top was breathtaking. I took (5) ___ of photos and put them all in an album when I got (6) ___.

gaps.rationale: 'Go on holiday' — 'on' is fixed with 'holiday'.
'The first time any of us had been there' — past perfect with 'been'.
'Watch the sun rise over the sea' — 'over' describes the direction of rising.
'The thing I remember most' — superlative adverb.
'Took lots/hundreds/loads of photos' — large quantity expressions.
'When I got home' or 'when I got back' — both express return.
